title: Rutte's Pro-Trump Stance on Iran Angers European Capitals — Financial Times
detail: The Financial Times reported that NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte's statements that European allies would 'ultimately unite' to heed Trump's call to deploy naval forces in the Strait of Hormuz drew significant criticism from officials in several European capitals. One diplomat told the FT that Rutte's remarks placed Europe 'in an extremely awkward and uncomfortable position.' The rift exposed deep divisions within NATO over how far the alliance should accommodate US demands — with Rutte regularly praised for appeasing Trump to keep the US inside NATO, but now facing pushback from the Europeans he ostensibly represents.

media.caption: Rutte's pro-Trump stance on Iran angers European capitals

title: Intra-NATO Split Over Hormuz Deepens as European Frustration Grows
detail: Ian Lesser of the German Marshall Fund described 'a growing frustration with the failure of various approaches to the administration in trying to repair what has been a very challenging transatlantic relationship over the last year,' spanning issues from trade to Greenland, climate policy, and Ukraine — calling it 'a broad-gauge crisis of trust.' Despite Rutte's diplomatic efforts, many European nations maintained that joining any Hormuz operation was conditional on the fighting with Iran ending first. The split was described as the most visible intra-NATO rupture since the 2003 Iraq War.

media.caption: Intra-NATO split over Hormuz deepens as European frustration grows
